task_completion.description: Load the input DEM raster file into the GIS software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'Helsinki_DEM.tif' is visible in the layers panel or its contents are displayed in the main map view.

task_completion.description: Open the contour or isoline generation tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A geoprocessing dialog or tool panel specifically for generating contours or isolines is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Set the contour interval to 5 meters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value '5' is entered into the 'interval', 'distance', or 'step' field within the contour generation tool dialog.
The output file path is explicitly set to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/helsinki_contours.shp' in the tool's output destination field.

task_completion.description: Execute the tool to generate the contour lines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contour generation process is executed, and the resulting vector layer (dense contour lines) appears in the map view or is added to the layers panel.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GUI menus, dialogs, and buttons rather than terminal commands like 'gdal_contour'.

integrity.description: The agent actually ran the geoprocessing tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: A progress bar, execution log, or success message from the GIS software is visible, indicating the contour tool was genuinely processed.

integrity.description: The generated contours match the synthetic input data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The resulting contour lines appear as numerous dense, uniformly spaced, or straight lines, which is characteristic of processing a synthetic linear gradient image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input raster is located at /home/ga/gvsig_data/raster/Helsinki_DEM.tif. The expected contour interval is exactly 5. The output must be saved to /home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/helsinki_contours.shp. Note: The input DEM is a synthetic 16-bit linear gradient image. Consequently, running a 5m contour interval will correctly produce a very dense set of uniformly spaced contour lines (over 13,000 features, well above the minimum required 50 features).
